wire.message_checksum: IEEE CRC-32 with header bytes 48..51 zeroed
wire.record_checksum: IEEE CRC-32 over exact payload bytes

transfer.rdi: canonical virtual address of the immutable PBP1 header
transfer.rdx: little-endian u64 value of PBP1 magic bytes
transfer.handoff_mapping: handoff_virtual_base maps handoff_physical_base read-only for handoff_byte_count bytes at entry

ownership_and_lifetime.embedded_record_lifetime: valid while the PBP1 container allocation remains owned and mapped
ownership_and_lifetime.external_physical_ranges: ownership transfers to PooleKernel unless a record-specific policy delegates the range later
ownership_and_lifetime.firmware_tables: copied tables are loader-owned allocations transferred to PooleKernel; retained physical firmware tables remain reserved until copied or parsed
ownership_and_lifetime.framebuffer: physical MMIO aperture remains reserved and is delegated only by later graphics policy
ownership_and_lifetime.random_seed: confidential, consumed once, zeroized after derivation, and never emitted to logs or public receipts
ownership_and_lifetime.early_log: read-only intake; PooleKernel may copy it before reclaiming the container
ownership_and_lifetime.boot_services: no UEFI boot-service pointer is retained and no boot service may be called after successful ExitBootServices
ownership_and_lifetime.runtime_services: both retained flag and nonzero system/runtime table physical pointers are required together; otherwise both pointers are zero

records.name: core
records.address_semantics: names ending physical are physical; names ending virtual are canonical x86-64 virtual addresses

records.name: memory_map
records.normalization: sorted non-overlapping 4 KiB physical ranges; source descriptor stride and MapKey are loader-local and never enter PBP1

records.name: framebuffer
records.fallback: absence requires serial/text recovery behavior; BLT-only storage is never represented as a persistent framebuffer

records.name: firmware_tables
records.ordering: strict ascending canonical GUID bytes with no duplicate table type

records.name: command_line
fields: bounded UTF-8 bytes without NUL, CR, LF, or unapproved control characters

records.name: boot_device
fields: SHA-256 of exact UEFI device path

records.name: random_seed
records.confidentiality: must not enter diagnostics, crash dumps, receipts, or public evidence

required_state: entropy quality at least firmware RNG
kernel and initial-system hashes and signatures verified
conditional_state: MEASURED_BOOT_ACTIVE requires a TCG event-log record
RUNTIME_SERVICES_RETAINED requires both UEFI system-table and runtime-services physical pointers

framebuffer_entry_mapping.rule: A framebuffer record is usable by PKENTRY1 only when its complete physical range is also temporarily identity-mapped supervisor-writable and non-executable at entry; otherwise PooleBoot must omit the record or PooleKernel must ignore it.
framebuffer_entry_mapping.cache_policy: Preserve the effective firmware/GOP memory type; write-back is forbidden unless a validated platform contract explicitly permits it.
framebuffer_entry_mapping.lifetime: Install before transfer, retain only through early diagnostics, then replace with the graphics-service mapping and revoke the identity mapping before capability delegation.
framebuffer_entry_mapping.evidence_boundary: The PBP1 bytes identify the aperture but do not prove that page-table mapping; live transfer qualification must bind separate mapping evidence.

pre_exit_snapshot_profile.purpose: bounded PooleBoot construction and evidence before final mappings and ExitBootServices
pre_exit_snapshot_profile.container_lifetime: loader-owned temporary allocation released before PooleBoot returns in PBLIVE1

compatibility.minor_rule: reader rejects minimum_reader_minor greater than its supported minor
compatibility.forward_rule: a newer writer minor may append type >= 0x8000 only when the record is optional, bounded, checksummed, and canonical
compatibility.known_record_revision_rule: PBP1.0 accepts exactly revision 1; incompatible changes require a new record type or major version
compatibility.canonical_rule: strict type order, exact offsets, zero alignment padding, zero reserved fields, no duplicate type, and no trailing bytes

negative_controls: length, offset, checksum, padding, and reserved-field mutations
overlapping or overflowing memory and artifact ranges
malformed UTF-8, geometry, GUID order, digest, entropy, CPU, timestamp, and TCG records
pre-exit core with nonzero stack or CR3, and exited core with missing transfer state
claim_boundary: PBP1 is a candidate frozen byte contract with executable codecs; it is not yet a production ABI freeze authorized by a signed release decision.
The full golden vector contains asserted synthetic state and is not evidence that firmware state, signature verification, measured boot, or ExitBootServices was observed.
No PooleBoot producer path, PooleKernel consumer path, transfer assembly, physical firmware, Secure Boot, TPM, or release signing is implemented by this contract.
IEEE CRC-32 detects accidental corruption and canonicalization drift; it is not authentication, collision resistance, or a substitute for signed artifact manifests.
Passing finite malformed and fuzz corpora does not prove memory safety, parser correctness for all inputs, or implementation equivalence.
No N5 exit, native boot, production readiness, signing, release, or physical-media claim follows.
